quietHours.notes: Sound level limits drop by 10 dB(A) during these hours where the receiving property is in a residential district.

decibelLimits.measurementNotes: Base residential limit is 55 dBA; nighttime/quiet-hours limits are reduced by 10 dB(A) from the daytime baseline, measured at the receiving property line.

enforcement.primary: Seattle Department of Construction & Inspections (SDCI) Noise Code enforcement
enforcement.secondary: Police non-emergency line for in-progress disturbances
enforcement.howToReport: For an in-progress disturbance, call the non-emergency police line. For a chronic issue, file with SDCI, which enforces the Noise Code.
